steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The study measured two things across 17 languages: syllable rate (varied widely) and information rate (stayed constant at ~39 bits per second). The correct choice must capture both findings — that one metric differed while the other remained the same.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — focuses only on a specific comparison or methodology: misses the key contrast between the two metrics. ✗
  • Choice B — addresses only part of the findings: does not capture that information rate stayed constant across all languages. ✗
  • Choice C — languages differed in syllables per second but conveyed information at roughly the same rate: accurately captures both findings — variation in syllable rate alongside a constant information rate. ✓
  • Choice D — claims a difference in information rates: contradicts the study's finding that information rate was approximately equal across all 17 languages. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
